pi_items.source: scripts/setup_opentoonz.sh downloads the opentoonz_sample repository from GitHub and extracts it to this path. Verified by downloading the zip and confirming the presence of dwanko_run.tnz.
tasks/import_bg_composite/setup_task.sh generates this image as a Sky Blue to Forest Green gradient at this exact path during task initialization.
setup_task.sh creates this directory, and export_result.sh checks it for the rendered PNG sequence. verifier.py also relies on results collected from this directory.
verifier.py uses this as the passing threshold. Checked dwanko_run.tnz and confirmed it contains 72 frames, making the 10 frame minimum achievable and correct.

verifier.py checks if the rendered output meets these color thresholds to ensure the background is present. A simulation of the gradient generated by setup_task.sh confirms top blue averages ~214 and bottom green averages ~145, validating these thresholds.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires compositing a synthetic background into the 'dwanko_run.tnz' OpenToonz scene, which is real sample data from the official OpenToonz repository, extracted to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz'. A synthetic gradient background (sky blue to forest green) is generated at '/home/ga/OpenToonz/assets/background_sky.png' during task setup. The expected output directory for the rendered frames is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/composite'. The scene has 72 frames, allowing for the 10-frame rendering minimum requirement. Verification checks will correctly pass if the top 20% of the image has a blue channel average > 150 and the bottom 20% has a green channel average > 80, confirming the background presence.
